questions.criteria: Identify the two main purposes highlighted in the cultural diversity passage.
key_concepts_expected: Achieving peace
Sustainable development

questions.criteria: Identify the correct tone option for the text passage.
key_concepts_expected: (D) hopeful and serious

questions.criteria: Explain how sustainable development can be achieved in approximately 30 words.
key_concepts_expected: Engaging in continuous intercultural dialogue
Acknowledging the world's natural and cultural diversity
Drawing upon creative potential from diverse cultures

questions.criteria: Explain the phrase 'cultural diversity is an asset' and evaluate the writer's tone in this line.
key_concepts_expected: Meaning: Diversity acts as a positive resource/value that bridges gaps between different nations
Tone description: Objective, confident, affirmative, or appreciative

questions.criteria: Identify the correct conceptual option from the passage.
key_concepts_expected: (D) Culture contributes to sustainable development

questions.criteria: Identify the specific vocabulary word matching the meaning 'to make known'.
key_concepts_expected: (B) affirm

questions.criteria: Identify the text resolution origin source.
key_concepts_expected: United Nations/UNESCO resolution regarding cultural diversity or sustainable development

questions.criteria: Explain what the author means by 'bridging the gap between cultures'.
key_concepts_expected: Fostering understanding, reducing divisions, and creating connection/harmony across diverse societies

questions.criteria: State the core objectives of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 mentioned in the text.
key_concepts_expected: Transforming the education system in India
Exposing at least 50% of learners to skill education by 2025
Overcoming the social status hierarchy associated with skill education

questions.criteria: Classify the nature of the statement 'India needs more skill education'.
key_concepts_expected: (B) an opinion, a subjective judgement

questions.criteria: Select the true objective option based on the text layout description.
key_concepts_expected: (C) Skill labs will allow other schools to use the facility

questions.criteria: State what setting up skill labs in schools will allow.
key_concepts_expected: Allowing neighboring or other schools to utilize the shared facility resources

questions.criteria: Locate the textual term corresponding to 'sense of self-worth or respect'.
key_concepts_expected: Dignity

questions.criteria: Identify the country with maximum student skill education allocation and provide relevant reasons based on data data charts.
key_concepts_expected: Accurate identification of the designated high-ranking nation
Logical justifications linking socio-economic policies or educational structure to skill priorities

questions.criteria: Identify the target target percentage of learners to be exposed to skill education by 2025.
Explain how NEP 2020 will build awareness and appreciation of Indian arts and artisanship.
key_concepts_expected: By integrating cultural education and crafts into institutions to emphasize the dignity of labor
Promoting various vocations related to local artisan styles

questions.criteria: Fill in the blank with the grammatically correct verb tense matching 'every day'.
key_concepts_expected: shares

questions.criteria: Locate structural syntax errors inside the context string 'The river turned and turned.'
key_concepts_expected: Identification of repetitive structure or tense alignment matching syntax tracking patterns

questions.criteria: Rewrite 'indeed it was always nice to be back there' applying precise capitalization and punctuation rules.
key_concepts_expected: Indeed, it was always nice to be back there.

questions.criteria: Choose the correct past tense option matching the temporal adverb 'yesterday'.
key_concepts_expected: (A) read

questions.criteria: Identify the correct singular possessive pronoun form to complete the sentence structure.
key_concepts_expected: its

questions.criteria: Identify the appropriate target modal verb representing obligation.
key_concepts_expected: (A) must

questions.criteria: Rearrange disordered word components into a coherent, grammatically sound sentence.
key_concepts_expected: He had been very occupied with work that week.

questions.criteria: Identify the correct option confirming the past tense state form of 'waited'.
key_concepts_expected: (D) waited

questions.criteria: Select the semantic proverb match option for 'To ___ is to care.'
key_concepts_expected: (A) share

questions.criteria: Draft a formal letter of enquiry as Soumya requesting summer baking course details.
key_concepts_expected: Correct formal letter layout (Sender address: 43-B, Shri Apartments, Prashant Lok; Recipient: Principal, Excellent Cooking School)
Clear statement of purpose referencing the advertisement date (25 February 20XX)
Inclusion of key requested parameters (course structure, duration, fee schema, timings, transport, internships)

questions.criteria: Write a 100-120 word analytical paragraph interpreting gym membership growth patterns and pandemic disruptions from 2010 to 2022.
key_concepts_expected: Highlighting the upward trend from 2010 to 2016 and the sharp acceleration in 2018
Detailing the severe contraction/drop in 2020 driven directly by the Covid-19 pandemic restrictions
Describing the subsequent post-pandemic recovery and resurgence trends observed by 2022

questions.criteria: Draw logical character inferences regarding Anne Frank based on her nursery farewell context extract.
key_concepts_expected: Demonstrates deep emotional attachments and sensitivity
Shows a affectionate relationship with her educators and institutions

questions.criteria: Identify the specific initial school environment where Anne's grandmother was involved.
key_concepts_expected: (C) Montessori Nursery School

questions.criteria: Identify Mrs. Kuperus and define her operational relationship with Anne Frank.
key_concepts_expected: She was the Headmistress of the Montessori Nursery School and acted as Anne's direct early childhood teacher/mentor

questions.criteria: Analyze what the structural literary phrase 'melancholic farewell' suggests about the event.
key_concepts_expected: Suggests a deeply sorrowful, sad, emotional separation filled with mutual grief and tears

questions.criteria: Identify the tone utilized by the poet in 'The Ball Poem' extract.
key_concepts_expected: (B) Sympathetic, understanding the depth of the boy's loss

questions.criteria: Explain the symbolic value of the 'Ball' and isolate the core figure of speech implemented.
key_concepts_expected: Symbolism: Represents innocent childhood days, early possessions, and the inevitable experience of loss
Figure of speech: Metaphor or Metonymy tracking transient life parameters

questions.criteria: Explain the significance of the text phrase 'All his young days into the harbour'.
key_concepts_expected: Refers to the boy's memories and years of carefree youth disappearing permanently along with the physical ball into the water

questions.criteria: Trace the boy's psychological journey from 'shaking grief' to 'learning' and define what this transition represents.
key_concepts_expected: Transition from paralyzing, raw emotional shock to rational acceptance of accountability/grief
Represents maturity, growth, and learning the fundamental epistemology of coping with real-world loss

questions.criteria: Elaborate how Robert Frost uses dark natural symbols (crow, hemlock) to spark a positive emotional transformation.
key_concepts_expected: The crow and hemlock tree traditionally represent gloom, doom, or poisonous omens
The sudden physical action of the crow shaking down the clean snow dust acts as a catalyst
Brings a sudden shifts in mood, saving the remaining part of the day from regret

questions.criteria: Justify how Custard the Dragon displays fearsome capabilities despite his submissive, cowardly reputation.
key_concepts_expected: Custard routinely cries for a safe cage while others boast of their bravery
When the pirate attacks, Custard shows fearsome traits: snorting like an engine, clashing his tail
He bravely attacks and swallows the pirate whole, proving his genuine physical valor

questions.criteria: Analyze the central theme of 'For Anne Gregory' comparing human attraction parameters with divine unconditional love.
key_concepts_expected: Human love is frequently superficial, based on external appearance (like yellow hair)
Divine love is unconditional and focused entirely on internal character, soul parameters, and authentic spiritual merit

questions.criteria: Synthesize Buddha's universal lessons regarding mortality, coping with loss, and the role of virtue in 'The Sermon at Benares'.
key_concepts_expected: Death is inevitable and universal for all living beings; grieving cannot restore the deceased
Surrendering selfishness and accepting mortality is the path to peace of mind
Good deeds and keeping an unbacked ethical focus provide comfort amidst transient suffering

questions.criteria: Provide examples to support the statement that Griffin was a brilliant scientist but a lawless, dangerous individual.
key_concepts_expected: Brilliance is proven by his discovery of a formula for human invisibility
Lawlessness is shown by his unethical actions: setting fire to his landlord's house, robbing a shop in Drury Lane, and stealing cash from the clergyman's desk
His misuse of science turns it into a dangerous tool of self-preservation and isolation rather than public welfare

questions.criteria: Trace Bholi's complete psychological transformation from an ignored simpleton into the self-respecting Sulekha under her teacher's care.
key_concepts_expected: Bholi initially suffers from stammering and pockmarks, and lacks confidence due to systemic neglect
The school teacher provides kindness, clear speech training, and builds her self-worth
The transformation culminates when she rejects the greedy Bishamber, speaking up with confidence as 'Sulekha'

questions.criteria: Compare and contrast childhood frameworks as described in 'A Baker from Goa' and Nelson Mandela's 'Long Walk to Freedom'.
key_concepts_expected: Similarities: Both reflect on a carefree period filled with simple joys (the aroma of bread vs. running in fields and roasting mealies)
Differences: The Goan narrator grew up in a stable, peaceful culture, whereas Mandela's childhood freedom was an illusion shaped by a systemic, racially oppressive apartheid regime

questions.criteria: Explain how Anil's quiet forgiveness and trust transformed Hari Singh in 'The Thief's Story'.
key_concepts_expected: Anil notices the damp stolen notes but chooses not to confront, scold, or turn Hari over to the police
By ignoring the crime and promising to teach him how to write full sentences, Anil breaks the cycle of deceit
Trust, sympathy, and the value of education prove far more powerful than punishment in reforming the young thief
